item.title: Better Coloring Pages
item.description:
Let’s say no to AI slop, especially when it comes to things our children experience. I will continue to add beautiful drawings and illustrations here as I find them.

  • Over 1,300 hand drawn illustrations of birds, mammals, insects, and fish scanned directly from The Naturalist’s Library. You’ll have to print these in black and white.

item.title: Revolution Medicines and Daraxonrasib
item.description:
Some pretty incredible cancer drug news came out last week. Revolution Medicines announced the results of their Phase 3 RASolute 302 clinical trial and their drug, daraxonrasib, demonstrated a median overall survival of 13.2 months versus 6.7 months for chemotherapy for their stage 4 pancreatic cancer patients.

If you’ve not seen it, I urge you to listen to this fantastic NY Times podcast episode between Ross Douthat and Ben Sasse. Sasse, recently diagnosed with Stage 4 pancreatic cancer, saw his tumors shrink 75% due to the drug.

item.title: A Revamp
item.description:
Simon Willison has been inspiring me lately.

It’s been nearly three years since I posted on this blog. Simon Willison posts to his blog every single day. I can do that, right?

I once heard a quote. It was something like… “In order to think, you need to write.” I don’t remember what it actually was, but the point stands. Writing is the best way to get to the bottom of your thoughts, and this is something I’d like to be better about doing.
